lesson.menu_title: The Writing Portfolio -- Review & Choosing the Right Format
lesson.menu_desc: Format-choice vocabulary + whether to / which to -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 20 -- The Writing Portfolio: Choosing the Right Format
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Eduarda | Aula 20 -- The Writing Portfolio

listenings.text: Hi Eduarda, congratulations on finishing the writing block. One last reminder, the hardest part is not the writing, it is choosing the right format. Before you write, decide whether to send an email, a memo, a set of minutes, or a status report. Think about your audience and your purpose. If it goes to one client, an email; if it informs the team, a memo; if it records a meeting, the minutes; if it updates stakeholders, a status report. Match the register and the tone to the reader. Then tailor the length, proofread it, and revise once before you send. You have built a real portfolio.

Hi team, a final note on professional writing. We now have four formats: the email, the memo, the minutes, and the status report. The skill is choosing which one to use. Always start from two questions, who is the audience, and what is the purpose. The format decides the register: formal for a client, neutral for the team. Then tailor the tone, keep it concise, proofread for errors, and revise before sending. A strong writer is not the one who writes the most, but the one who knows whether to write at all, and in what format.

extra_audio.text: Eduarda first identified the audience and the purpose. She decided which format to use. She chose the right register and tone for the reader. She tailored the length and proofread the text. She revised it once and then sent it.
